name: WP-arch-correctness-races
description: Three lost-update / non-atomic concurrency races where a read-compute-write can lose a concurrent update. F-169 — reviewVocabulary SM-2 update in services/vocabulary.ts: the wrapping transaction does not provide the isolation it appears to claim, so two concurrent reviews of the same vocab item can lose one update (corrupts SM-2 scheduling state: ease/interval/due). F-170 — pending celebration writes in services/celebrations.ts can lose concurrent updates. F-171 — celebration writes: the READ happens OUTSIDE the SELECT FOR UPDATE lock, so the lock does not actually protect the read-compute-write window. Fixes require precise lock-scope / isolation / atomic-SQL reasoning — Opus plan-phase, Sonnet implements.
acceptanceCriteria: Each race closed so concurrent same-entity requests cannot lose an update — read-compute-write made atomic via correct lock scope (read INSIDE the SELECT FOR UPDATE / row lock), an appropriate isolation level, or an atomic SQL update. MANDATORY red-green concurrency break test per variant (F-169 SM-2, F-170 pending-celebration, F-171 read-outside-lock): each reproduces the lost update against the PRE-FIX code (demonstrated RED via revert/stash — write test -> pass -> revert fix -> fail -> restore) and passes after. No internal mocks (GC1/GC6) — exercise the real DB path. Outcome: under concurrent same-row writes the final persisted state reflects BOTH updates (no lost update); no new deadlock. Confirm exact current sites by reading services/vocabulary.ts (reviewVocabulary) and services/celebrations.ts before planning.
riskImpact: P1 — silent data corruption under concurrency. Lost SM-2 updates degrade spaced-repetition scheduling correctness (wrong intervals/due dates); lost celebration/progress updates corrupt progress state. Failures are silent and hard to detect post-hoc, eroding learning-state integrity. Concurrency fixes are subtle (lock ordering / isolation) — a mis-fix risks deadlock or false confidence, hence the Opus plan-phase + mandatory negative-path proof.
bodyText:
Completion Summary — 2026-06-13
What was done:
Closed the three lost-update / non-atomic concurrency races in WI-717 (WP-arch-correctness-races) — F-169 (reviewVocabulary SM-2), F-170 (pending-celebration writes), F-171 (celebration read outside the SELECT FOR UPDATE lock). Each read-compute-write was made atomic under a row lock, and each shipped with a red-green concurrency break test exercising the real Neon DB (no internal mocks; GC1/GC6 clean). Delivered on PR #1126, squash-merged to main as d99562583; advisory Claude review APPROVED with 0 must-fix / 0 should-fix.
What changed:
- apps/api/src/services/vocabulary.ts (F-169): reviewVocabulary wrapped a transaction but took no lock on the retention-card read, so under READ COMMITTED two concurrent reviews of the same card read the same SM-2 state and the later commit overwrote the earlier (lost ease/interval/repetitions/consecutiveSuccesses). Fix: ensureVocabularyRetentionCard remains the idempotent bootstrap, then acquire SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the retention card inside the existing transaction and compute from the locked row.
- apps/api/src/services/celebrations.ts + apps/api/src/services/home-surface-cache.ts (F-170 + F-171): queueCelebration read+merged pending celebrations OUTSIDE the lock (F-171) and wrote a precomputed array that bypassed the locked row's current value (F-170). Fix: a new computePending reducer (PendingCelebrationsReducer) threaded through writeHomeSurfacePendingCelebrations -> mergeHomeSurfaceCacheData(InTx) runs the read-dedup-append INSIDE the row lock, receiving the locked row so dedup reads celebrationsSeenByChild from locked state. Post-review hardening: queuedAt is now stamped inside the locked reducer (TOCTOU fix from CodeRabbit) so a concurrent markCelebrationsSeen cannot make the appended entry land with queuedAt <= seenAt and be hidden.
- apps/api/src/services/vocabulary.test.ts: hand-rolled mock DB extended with a .for() handler for the locked read (no jest.mock — not a GC1/GC6 target).
- New real-DB break tests: apps/api/src/services/vocabulary.concurrency.integration.test.ts, apps/api/src/services/celebrations.concurrency.integration.test.ts.
- .github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml: synced byte-identical to origin/main so the pinned advisory Claude review could run on this PR (tooling-only, no logic change).
Verification:
Red-green proven by stash-revert-rerun against pre-fix code: F-169 8 concurrent reviews -> consecutiveSuccesses=2 (RED) -> 8 (GREEN); F-170 5 concurrent distinct appends -> 1 persisted (RED) -> 5 + 5 celebration_events (GREEN); F-171 10 concurrent (two waves) -> 1 persisted (RED) -> 10 + 10 celebration_events (GREEN). The fix also flipped the pre-existing BUG-467 integration test (already failing on main) to green. CodeRabbit substantively reviewed and its three findings were triaged in-thread and fixed (TOCTOU queuedAt stamp; queue-vs-seen regression test; per-run unique seed suffix). Advisory Claude review APPROVED 0/0. Local gates green pre-merge: api unit suites (vocabulary.test.ts, celebrations.test.ts), 9 integration cases across 4 suites, tsc --build clean, eslint clean. Deterministic CI gates passed on the merged head.
Caveats / Follow-ups:
No schema change / no migration (both unique constraints already existed). Lock ordering is safe — each path holds exactly one lockable row (vocabulary_retention_cards keyed by profile-scoped vocabularyId; coaching_card_cache keyed by profileId) and the two paths never share a transaction, so no deadlock. Pre-existing, out-of-scope: the opportunistic pruning write in getPendingCelebrations runs without a held lock (an F-164-class stale-write gap) — not part of this WI; flag for a future capture if it matters. The adversarial-review [BLOCKER] about the retention-card unique constraint on vocabularyId alone was resolved as a non-issue (vocabulary.profileId is notNull FK, so vocabularyId is profile-scoped 1:1).
